decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.29°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective and conservative first step is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader material to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hot spot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 1.5x enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local temperature gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though not modeled here.
